title: Anti-Fragility

content.statement: Some systems get stronger under stress.
content.question: What challenge are you avoiding that would actually make you stronger?
sop: List the last 3 setbacks or failures you experienced.
For each, note whether you changed the underlying system afterward, or just recovered and moved on unchanged.
Rate your Stress exposure and Adaptation response each 1-10 (see calculation, page 6), how much real challenge you faced, and how much you actually adjusted because of it.
Choose one controlled stress to introduce deliberately (a hard conversation, a stretch goal, a new skill under pressure).
After it happens, document what you'd change in the system, not just what you personally learned.
questions: What challenge are you avoiding that would actually make you stronger?
Think of the last real setback you had, did you emerge with a better system, or the same one that failed the first time?
What's one stress you could deliberately introduce this month, on your terms, before life introduces it on its own?

definitions.def: A system that gets stronger under stress.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10: Stress (how much real challenge you faced), Adaptation (how much you actually changed your approach because of it). Stress 7 times Adaptation 8 equals 56. Higher means the setback made the system stronger; lower means you absorbed the hit but nothing actually improved.
content.closing_statement: Let stress upgrade the system, not just pass through.

caption:
Principle Nº 185: Anti-Fragility

Some systems get stronger under stress.

Ask yourself: What challenge are you avoiding that would actually make you stronger?
